features.name: In-Cell Curve Fitting
features.description: Automatically fits mathematical models (e.g. 4PL dose-response) to series of data points and renders interactive fitted curves directly inside grid cells.
features.evidence_summary: The Fit cellRenderer, Curve fitting descriptor, and dose-response workflow doc all directly implement and describe in-grid curve fitting; the init and semTypeDetector are infra that exists solely to enable it.

features.name: Data-to-Curves Transformation
features.description: Converts raw tabular data (concentration, readout, and metadata columns) into a curve-encoded dataframe with per-compound fitted curves, accessible from the Data top menu.
features.evidence_summary: Both functions share the 'dataToCurves' name root and represent the same pipeline (bulk transform + its menu entry), tracked consistently as 'Data-to-Curves' across multiple changelog versions.

features.name: Multi-Curve Viewer
features.description: A standalone Datagrok viewer that superimposes multiple in-cell curves on one chart, with configurable axes, legend, and outlier display.
features.evidence_summary: The viewer function and its dedicated help page share the exact name 'Multi curve viewer'; changelog entries track its parameter evolution (showOutliers, legendColumnName, min/max axes) as a distinct viewer feature.

features.name: Curve Format Import
features.description: Imports fitted curves from external file formats — GraphPad Prism PZFX, XML 3DX, and compact dose-response JSON — via a pluggable converter registry, with full file preview and handler support for PZFX.
features.evidence_summary: All three converter functions share the 'curveConverter' role and identical output type; the PZFX viewer and handler extend this into full file-handling for GraphPad Prism, with changelog noting 'Added PZFX file viewer and handler' and 'Added dynamically extendable format support'.

features.name: Curve Statistics Columns
features.description: Extracts statistical parameters (e.g. IC50, Hill slope) from fitted curves and appends them as new dataframe columns, with both per-row and aggregated variants.
features.evidence_summary: Both transform-role functions append curve-derived statistics to a dataframe and differ only in aggregation level, making them two facets of the same statistics-extraction capability.

features.name: Minimum Significant Ratio (MSR)
features.description: Server-side Python analysis that computes the Minimum Significant Ratio for compounds from IC50 values, run dates, and assay metadata to assess inter-run assay reproducibility.
features.evidence_summary: The Calculate MSR script and its changelog entries ('Added MSR script', 'Updated MSR env') consistently describe this as a self-contained reproducibility-analysis capability distinct from curve fitting itself.

features.name: Assay Plates
features.description: Visualizes and manages assay plate data including well-level concentration, layout, and readout information for drug-screening workflows.
features.evidence_summary: The 'Assay Plates' function (concentration/layout/readout data) and the Plates package docs share a well/plate theme corroborated by changelog entries on plate rendering and well generation.
